entries.d: To think about and organise a holiday or journey
entries.s: We are _____ a trip to sunny Italy for the summer holidays — deciding which cities to visit and how many days to stay in each.

entries.d: To reserve and pay for a trip in advance
entries.s: My parents _____ a holiday to Spain last week at the travel agency — they paid in advance for flights and a hotel for five nights.

entries.d: To reserve a room at a place where travellers sleep
entries.s: Don't forget to _____ a hotel reservation online before we leave so we definitely have a room when we arrive at night.

entries.d: To pay to use a vehicle for a short time
entries.s: We're going to _____ a car for one week at the airport when we arrive in Spain so we can drive to different beaches.

entries.w: to check the area out online
entries.d: To look at information about a place on the internet
entries.s: Let's _____ the area out online with Google Maps before we go on holiday to see the streets and find interesting places.

entries.d: To get a book that explains words in another language
entries.s: She _____ a small travel dictionary at the bookshop to help her look up new French words during her trip to Paris.

entries.w: to find out about good restaurants
entries.d: To search for nice places to eat in the area
entries.s: We need to _____ about good local restaurants with great reviews near our hotel before we go out hungry for dinner tonight.

entries.w: to find information about the best beaches
entries.g: Informationen über die besten Strände finden
entries.d: To search for details on the nicest places to swim and sunbathe
entries.s: She went online to _____ information about the best sandy beaches with clear blue water for her summer holiday in Greece.
a: find information about the best beaches
mc: to stay away from the water

entries.w: to look at a map of the area
entries.g: sich eine Karte der Gegend ansehen
entries.d: To study a diagram of the region to find your way
entries.s: Let's _____ a paper map of the area together so we don't get lost in the narrow winding streets of the old town.
a: look at a map of the area

entries.w: to find out what to do there
entries.g: herausfinden, was man dort machen kann
entries.d: To learn about activities and things to see at a place
entries.s: Before our exciting trip, let's _____ what to do there — museums, parks, beaches, restaurants, everything we can explore.
a: find out what to do there

entries.d: A small insect or creature
entries.s: There's a tiny red _____ with black spots on the window — I think it's a ladybird insect that flew in from outside.

entries.d: To like one thing more than another
entries.s: My mum _____ tea to coffee in the morning — she thinks tea is better and drinks three cups of it every single day.

entries.d: The main tongue used by the government of a country
entries.s: English is the _____ of many countries around the world like the UK, USA, Australia, and Ireland where it's used in government.

entries.d: A small area outside a window, usually on a higher floor
entries.s: We sat on the hotel _____ attached to our fourth-floor room and watched the beautiful sunset over the sea with a drink.

entries.d: Something that is against the law
entries.s: Stealing money or goods from a shop without paying is a serious _____ that can be punished by the police with a fine or prison.

entries.d: To make a hole in the ground
entries.s: The playful dog loves to _____ deep holes in the soft garden soil with his paws and bury his bones underground.

entries.d: Very surprised and upset by something bad
entries.s: Everyone was absolutely _____ when they heard the surprising sad news about the serious car accident on the motorway.

entries.d: A creature that lives freely in nature, not kept by humans
entries.s: You should never ever try to touch a _____ like a bear or a wolf — they can be very dangerous and hurt you with their teeth.

entries.d: A large area of land that is very wet, often with plants and animals
entries.s: Many waterbirds like ducks, herons, and flamingos live in the _____ areas full of reeds near the river in our nature reserve.

entries.d: If not, or else
entries.s: You should leave home now for the station, _____ you'll definitely miss the last bus at 8 pm and have to walk home in the rain.

entries.d: Filled with material to keep its shape (like a dead animal made to look alive)
entries.s: There was a _____ brown bear with glass eyes in the natural history museum — it looked very real and scary but wasn't alive.

entries.d: Wild animals and plants living in their natural environment
entries.s: This protected national park is famous for its amazing _____ — you can see bears, wolves, deer, eagles, and many other wild animals.

entries.d: Immediately, right now
entries.s: Come here right now _____! I really need your urgent help in the kitchen because I'm burning something on the stove.

entries.d: A part of a tree that grows out from the main trunk
entries.s: The small blue bird was sitting on a thin _____ high up in the tall oak tree that stretched out from the main trunk.

entries.d: Used to add something new to a conversation
entries.s: Oh, _____, did you hear the news that we have a surprise maths test tomorrow morning at 8 am? The teacher just announced it.

entries.d: Not having good manners, rude
entries.s: It's very _____ to interrupt other people rudely while they are speaking — you should always wait politely for your turn to talk.

entries.g: um eine Ecke, um eine Kurve
entries.d: Around a curve in the road
entries.s: The red sports car disappeared quickly _____ in the winding mountain road and we couldn't see it any more on the straight part.

entries.d: A short, thick plant with many branches
entries.s: The shy black cat was hiding quietly behind the big thick green leafy _____ in the corner of the garden to watch birds.

entries.d: A wound made by something sharp
entries.s: He got a small painful _____ on his finger from the sharp edge of the paper — a tiny drop of blood came out.

entries.d: To leave quickly in a car
entries.s: The taxi _____ quickly from the pavement before I could get my bag from the back seat — I had to shout to stop it.

entries.d: The part of a car or machine that makes it move
entries.s: The car _____ under the bonnet made a strange loud clunking noise, so we stopped immediately on the side of the road to check.

entries.d: The place next to the driver where a passenger sits
entries.s: I always sit in the _____ next to the driver because I get bad car sickness if I sit in the back with my sister.

entries.d: A person who works in a nature reserve and looks after it
entries.s: The friendly _____ in the green uniform at the national park told us not to feed the wild squirrels and deer with human food.

entries.d: Red, painful skin caused by too much sun
entries.s: I forgot to put on sun cream at the beach and got a bad red painful _____ all over my shoulders and back.

entries.d: To flip upside down or rotate completely
entries.s: The small boat _____ completely in the strong waves during the storm and everyone wearing life jackets fell into the cold water.

entries.d: To speak very quietly so only one person can hear
entries.s: She _____ the test answer very quietly to me behind her hand so the strict teacher at the front of the room wouldn't hear us.

entries.d: An accident when a vehicle hits something hard
entries.s: There was a terrible car _____ with two damaged vehicles on the busy motorway this morning and the police had to close the road.
